title: Trisetopsis longa
taxonomy: Trisetopsis longa is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. The genus Trisetopsis is closely related to the genus Trisetum, with which it shares similarities in morphology and anatomy. Further classification of Trisetopsis longa can be made at the infraspecific level, where it may be divided into subspecies or varieties. However, without more specific information, the infraspecific classification of Trisetopsis longa is not known.

intro: Trisetopsis longa, also known as Long Spike Grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of China and the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. Bioactive constituents of Trisetopsis longa include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Trisetopsis longa include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as traditional Chinese medicine formulations such as capsules and tablets, which are often used to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
